response.text: I kept your blue ticket stub in the book by my bed. Now the pages smell like rain and the words that you said. Your glass still has a ring on the ring around the rim. I wash it, put it back like you might walk in. But the door stays shut and the floor stays cold. I hear your name in the pipes below. I tell myself it won't hurt long, and the whole damn room proves me wrong. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. I'm still here singing this one song. I try to let go. It pulls me under slow. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. Your shoes by the heater never learned to leave. I trip on the laces when I cross the room. The little green plant you forgot on the sill keeps leaning to the glass like it's waiting still. But the door stays shut and the floor stays cold. I hear your name in the pipes below. I tell myself it won't hurt long, and the whole damn room proves me wrong. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. I'm still here singing this one song. I try to let go. It pulls me under slow. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. If I write it down, maybe it turns to dust. If I say your name, maybe it won't cut. But the ink runs dark and the night runs long. I keep reaching out to where you belong. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. I'm still here singing this one song. I try to let go. It pulls me under slow. I'm still here. You're gone, gone, gone. Still here. Still here alone.
